_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to sketch linear graphs in various forms and solve linear equations.

_source.w: We are learning to sketch linear graphs in various forms and solve linear equations.
y: Being able to move fluently between different forms of linear equations (gradient-intercept, general form, point-gradient) gives us flexibility in problem-solving.
Sketching graphs quickly using intercepts and gradient is a practical skill for visualising relationships.
Linear equations and their graphs model real-world relationships in science, economics, engineering and everyday contexts.
eg: Sketching y = –2x + 5 by identifying the y-intercept (5) and gradient (–2).
Converting 3x + 2y = 12 to y = –1.5x + 6 and sketching using intercepts.
Finding the equation of a line passing through (2, 5) with gradient 3.
Determining whether a point lies on a given line by substitution.
